events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: unsealed weatherhead penetration on a residential service entrance, work in-progress. Need to assess hazards, severity, controls. NEC Art. 230 covers service entrances. Weatherhead (service head) must be weatherproof/raintight. Unsealed penetration allows water ingress into service raceway/conduit, potentially into meter/panel — water damage, corrosion, short/arc/fire hazard, and energized conductors exposed to moisture. Severity high. Decision fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60. Confidence high.

    "Stop work and de-energize/isolate the service if moisture has entered energized equipment",
    "Seal the weatherhead penetration with approved weatherproof sealing means per manufacturer instructions",
    "Inspect and dry/replace any wet conductors, terminals, or meter components before re-energizing",
    "Verify raintight/weatherproof integrity of the service entrance assembly before acceptance"

  "rationale": "An unsealed weatherhead penetration violates the weatherproof/raintight requirements for service entrance conductors and fittings under NEC Art. 230, creating a high risk of water ingress into energized service metering and equipment, which can lead to corrosion, arcing, shorts, and fire. Work must fail until the penetration is properly sealed and the assembly verified weather-tight.",
